Students for a Better Future — Shaping Extraordinary Minds

SBF High School Programme

Hi —
you are part of
something new.

SBF believes that learning should feel personal — connected to real life, not just a classroom. What you and your child did today is part of that vision.

But today is just the beginning. SBF is building a world where your child has access to learning that moves at their pace, meets them where they are, and is built around what actually matters in their real life. Interactive. Honest. Made for them.

What you are about to experience is a small glimpse of that. This one is for you.

Thrive Day 1 2026

Your Child Had
An Extraordinary Day.

While your child was in their session, they looked at their school results, set a goal for this term, and learned about their energy and wellness. This app is your guide — the place where you can both learn about the hormones that shape how you feel.

You are part of the SBF family.

SBF supports your child — not just with school, but with the tools and self-belief they need for the future. Today is part of that. You being here is too.

How This Works

Work through each tab at your own pace. The most important part is the Connect tab at the end. That is where the real conversation with your child begins.

Before we begin

How Are
You Doing?

Three honest questions — for you, not your child.

🔒
This is just for you.
Nothing you enter here is linked to your name. Your answers are anonymous. Be honest — that is the only way this works.
How would you honestly rate today?
One word for how you are carrying things right now.
One thing you want to do differently at home after today.

This is what happened

What Your Child
Did Today.

Swipe through each card to see what happened in your child's sessions today.

🎯
Academics

Your child looked at their real school results — not the version they may have shared with you. They looked honestly at which subjects are going well and which ones need more work.

They then wrote a SMART goal for this term — something Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant and Time-bound. A real target with a real deadline, not just "I will try harder."

For many of them, this was the first time they had been honest about a subject that is not going well.

Try this: Ask them to show you their SMART goal. Not to check it or judge it — just to know it. You can say: "I want to know what you are working toward."
Tap card to continue →
⚡
Wellness

Your child did an Energy Mapping exercise — looking honestly at which areas of their life give them energy and which ones drain it. Physical, emotional, social, academic, and more.

They shared their map with another student and talked about what is working and what feels hard. They also learned a simple breathing technique — star breathing — to help them calm down before trying to solve a problem.

They were reminded they are not alone, and that asking for help early makes a real difference. They chose one action to take this term to improve their balance.

Try this: Ask them to share their energy map with you. Then share yours. Ask: "What is one thing we can improve together this term?"
Tap card to continue →
💬
The Connection Card

At the end of their session, your child was given four sentences — things they might want to say to you but have not found the words for yet. They were just given the words.

You have matching questions in the Connect tab. Between the two cards, a conversation is already halfway started.

Try this: Put your phone down for ten minutes. Ask one question. Then just listen. You do not need to fix anything.
✓ All done
Academic Literacy

Reading Your
Child's Report.

Understanding your child's report helps you have the right conversation when it arrives. A number without context is just a number. This section helps you understand what the marks really mean.

Start with the small wins. Look first for a subject that improved, a mark at or above the class average, or a teacher comment that mentions effort. Start the conversation there — before the red lights.
CAPS Achievement Levels
Class average matters. 58% against a class average of 52% is above average. The same 58% against 72% is below. Always ask for context before reacting.

"Promoted" means your child met all requirements. "Progressed" means the system moved them forward even if some requirements were not met. The gaps are still there.

A student can only repeat a year once in Grades 7–9. After that they move forward automatically. This is mid-year. There is still time. A bad mark now is a warning, not a final answer.

This pass lets your child study for a degree at university. For Grade 12 they need: 40% Home Language, 40% First Additional Language, 50% Mathematics (or 40% Maths Literacy), 50% in four other subjects.

Pure Maths keeps more doors open than Maths Literacy. The habits built now in Grades 8 and 9 directly shape what is possible in Grade 12.

O
Outstanding — "Your teacher says your effort is outstanding. Tell me what that looks like."
G
Good — "Good effort — which subject do you work hardest in?"
S
Satisfactory — "You are meeting expectations. Where could you push a bit more?"
N
Needs improvement — "I want to understand what is getting in the way — not to judge you."
U
Unsatisfactory — "Something is wrong — not with you, with the situation. Let's figure it out together."

Not all schools use this system. Check the legend on the report — usually at the bottom or on the back.

The Week Ahead

Start A
Conversation.

Your child has a matching card with their own prompts. Pick one question. Ask it when the moment is calm. Then stop and listen.

①
"What was one moment today where you felt proud of yourself — even something small?"
②
"If you could change one thing about how school feels right now, what would it be?"
③
"What is something you wish I understood better about what your days are like?"
④
"Is there anything you have been carrying lately that you haven't known how to say?"
← swipe or use arrows to browse →
The one rule: Ask one question. Then stop talking. Just listen. You do not have to fix anything in that moment. Presence is the point.

My commitment for this week

One specific thing you will do differently in the next two weeks.

You came today. You sat with hard things. You thought about your own story and your child's. That is not nothing. That is everything.

Chose a conversation worth having

Thank you for being here today. SBF exists because of parents like you — parents who show up, who want more for their children, and who are willing to do the harder work of understanding. Your engagement is not a small thing. It is the foundation everything else is built on.

— The SBF Team

A Closing Message on Balance

Talk with your child about balance. There will always be things that fill you and things that drain you — we need to look at how to balance this out all the time. This isn't a one-time fix. There is no point in only working on one aspect of wellness while everything else fails. All dimensions — physical, emotional, social, intellectual, spiritual, environmental, and career — need attention working together.

SBF Is
Always Here.

The hard terms. The uncertain reports. The moments you are not sure what to say. You are part of a community of parents doing something extraordinary — choosing presence over pressure, and connection over correction.

We do not do this alone. Neither does your child.
